div1.entryFree: Cheveril (Fr. cuir de chevreuil) roebuck-leather; symbol of flexibility: a sentence is but a c. glove to a good wit: how quickly the wrong side may be turned outward! Tw. III, 1, 13. which gifts . . . the capacity of your soft c. conscience would receive, if you might please to stretch it, H8 II, 3, 32. here's a wit of c., that stretches from an inch narrow to an ell broad, Rom. II, 4, 87.
